source: ceramics-foundation/chemistry/material-safety.md, FDA Compliance Policy Guides, OSHA, NIOSH

toxic_materials.health_effects: Cumulative poison affecting nervous system, especially children. Learning disabilities, developmental delays, neurological damage.
toxic_materials.leaching_threshold: FDA has specific extraction limits per category
toxic_materials.notes: NEVER use in functional ware. No amount of washing or boiling removes lead from pottery.

toxic_materials.health_effects: Known human carcinogen. Kidney damage, bone demineralization, respiratory irritation.
toxic_materials.leaching_threshold: FDA strict leaching limits for ceramicware
toxic_materials.notes: Used in red/orange/yellow colorants. Can fume at firing temperatures.

toxic_materials.health_effects: Poisonous if ingested. Violent diarrhea, convulsive tremors, muscular paralysis.
toxic_materials.leaching_threshold: EPA drinking water: 2.0 mg/L. Safe leach: 1 mg/L.
toxic_materials.notes: Low risk in properly fired ware (converts to insoluble barium sulfate). Avoid in food-contact surfaces.

toxic_materials.health_effects: Known human carcinogen. Lung cancer, skin ulcers, kidney/liver damage.
toxic_materials.notes: Chromium III (Cr2O3) is relatively safe. Know your chromium form.

toxic_materials.health_effects: Probable human carcinogen. Skin sensitization, respiratory irritation.
toxic_materials.notes: Avoid skin contact. Respirator for dry powders.

toxic_materials.health_effects: Toxic if ingested. Nausea, vomiting, neurological effects, thyroid effects.
toxic_materials.notes: Used as flux. Avoid ingestion of raw material.

toxic_materials.leaching_threshold: Specific OSHA PEL exists due to neurotoxicity
toxic_materials.notes: Can fume at firing temperatures — kiln ventilation mandatory.

safe_colorants.notes: Safe in all forms (red, yellow, black, brown)
Safe in fired ware. Toxic in raw form but used in low percentages.

acid_test.purpose: Test if a glaze is food-safe by checking for leaching under acidic conditions
procedure: Squeeze fresh lemon juice onto the glazed surface
Let sit for 24 hours in a warm location
Wipe clean and examine the glaze surface
Changes in color, gloss, or surface texture indicate leaching

interpretation.no_change: Likely food-safe (not guaranteed — lab testing is definitive)
interpretation.surface_change: Glaze is being attacked — NOT food-safe

acid_test.limitations: Home acid test only detects gross leaching. Laboratory testing (ICP-MS) is required for definitive results.

leaching_risk_factors.description: Glaze not fully matured — significantly higher leaching risk
Cracks in glaze create pathways for acid attack and bacterial growth
High Na2O/K2O glazes are more soluble
Thick glaze may not fully mature, especially at interior
Tomato, vinegar, citrus — higher leaching risk

kiln_unloading: Safety glasses for viewing above orange heat
